sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy is indicated for advanced symptomatic KS, limited cutaneous disease that is symptomatic or cosmetically bothersome, and relapsed/refractory disease. First-line options include liposomal doxorubicin for non-transplant KS and sirolimus for transplant KS. Subsequent options include nivolumab, pembrolizumab, pomalidomide, and other agents. Corticosteroid pre-medication should generally be avoided [KS-F, MS-13].

cards.name: Liposomal Doxorubicin
drugs: Liposomal Doxorubicin 20 mg/m2 IV every 2 to 3 weeks
cards.detail: Setting: First-line non-transplant KS; relapsed/refractory. Drugs: Liposomal Doxorubicin 20 mg/m2 IV every 2 to 3 weeks. Cycle days: 21–28 days. Key toxicities: Cardiotoxicity (perform echocardiogram prior and periodically); neutropenia, nausea [KS-F, footnote d].

cards.name: Paclitaxel
cards.detail: Setting: First-line non-transplant KS; relapsed/refractory. Drugs: Paclitaxel 100 mg/m2 IV weekly; 100 mg/m2 IV every 2 weeks; 100 mg/m2 IV every 3 weeks; 135 mg/m2 IV every 3 weeks. Cycle days: 7–21 days depending on schedule. Key toxicities: Neutropenia, peripheral neuropathy [KS-F, 133].

cards.name: Sirolimus
drugs: Sirolimus Loading dose 0.15 mg/kg PO followed by 0.04–0.06 mg/kg/day to maintain trough levels 6–10 ng/mL
cards.detail: Setting: First-line transplant KS; useful in certain circumstances for relapsed/refractory. Drugs: Loading dose 0.15 mg/kg PO followed by 0.04–0.06 mg/kg/day to maintain trough levels 6–10 ng/mL or 2 mg PO daily. Cycle days: Continuous. Key toxicities: Requires monitoring of trough levels; DDIs with ART [KS-F, footnote e].

cards.name: Nivolumab
cards.detail: Setting: Relapsed/refractory. Drugs: Nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks. Cycle days: 28 days. Key toxicities: Immune-related adverse events; avoid in MCD/KICS [KS-F, footnote i].

cards.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for up to 6 months
cards.detail: Setting: Relapsed/refractory. Drugs: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for up to 6 months. Cycle days: 21 days. Key toxicities: Immune-related adverse events; avoid in MCD/KICS [KS-F, footnote i].

cards.name: Pomalidomide
drugs: Pomalidomide 4 or 5 mg/day PO days 1–21 of each 28-day cycle
cards.detail: Setting: Relapsed/refractory. Drugs: Pomalidomide 4 or 5 mg/day PO days 1–21 of each 28-day cycle. Cycle days: 28 days. Key toxicities: Neutropenia, infection, edema; REMS program required [KS-F, footnote l].

timeline.title: 1st Line
timeline.body: First-line for non-transplant KS: Liposomal Doxorubicin (superior response rates compared to older regimens; well-tolerated [KS-F, 121,122]). Alternatives: Paclitaxel [KS-F]. First-line for transplant KS: Sirolimus (mTOR inhibitor that may allow reduction of other immunosuppressants; effective in transplant-associated KS [KS-F, 21]). Alternatives: Liposomal Doxorubicin, Paclitaxel [KS-F]. Biomarker requirements: For PWH on ART, consult ID pharmacist due to DDIs [KS-F].

timeline.title: Relapsed/Refractory
timeline.body: Relapsed/refractory after first-line: Nivolumab, Pembrolizumab, or Pomalidomide (nivolumab ORR 40% in HIV-related KS [KS-F, 143]; pembrolizumab ORR 71% in classic/endemic KS [KS-F, 144]; pomalidomide ORR 60% in HIV-related KS [KS-F, 72]). Alternatives: albumin-bound paclitaxel, bortezomib, etoposide, gemcitabine, imatinib, ipilimumab + nivolumab, lenalidomide, sirolimus (for transplant KS), thalidomide (for IRIS), vinorelbine [KS-F]. Biomarker requirements: ICIs should not be used in MCD or KICS due to risk of flare [KS-F, footnote i].
